---
title: "Test 01: Differential Expression Analysis"
output: html_document
---

## Goal

This test focuses on the main differential expression analysis workflow in the project.

Students should practice:

- reading DEG result files
- creating new columns from existing columns
- using logical conditions to classify genes
- reproducing the main ideas from the DEG and volcano scripts

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
cur <- tryCatch(knitr::current_input(), error = function(e) NULL)
path <- if (is.null(cur)) NULL else if (is.character(cur)) cur else cur[["path"]]
if (!is.null(path) && length(path) > 0) {
  project_root <- normalizePath(file.path(dirname(path[1]), ".."), mustWork = FALSE)
} else {
  wd <- getwd()
  project_root <- if (dir.exists(file.path(wd, "Data"))) wd else normalizePath(file.path(wd, ".."), mustWork = FALSE)
}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = TRUE)
library(dplyr)
deg_res <- read.csv(file.path(project_root, "Results", "02_DEG", "Tumor_vs_normal.csv"), row.names = 1)
```

## Question 1

Inspect the DEG result table.

Tasks:

- print `dim(deg_res)`
- print the first 6 column names
- show the first 3 rows of `deg_res[, 1:5]`

```{r q1}
# Guidance code:
# dim(deg_res)
# names(deg_res)
# head(deg_res[, 1:5], 3)

# TODO
```

## Question 2

Create a new column called `log10FDR`.

Rule:

- `log10FDR = -log10(adj.P.Val)`

```{r q2}
# Guidance code:
# deg_res$log10FDR <- -log10(deg_res$adj.P.Val)
# deg_res$DEG <- "NotSig"
# deg_res$DEG[deg_res$logFC > 1 & deg_res$adj.P.Val < 0.05] <- "Up"
# deg_res$DEG[deg_res$logFC < -1 & deg_res$adj.P.Val < 0.05] <- "Down"
# table(deg_res$DEG)

# TODO
```

## Question 3

Keep only significant genes and show the top 5 genes with the largest positive `logFC`.

Tasks:

- create `deg_only` using `DEG != "NotSig"`
- print `dim(deg_only)`
- print the top 5 rows sorted by `desc(logFC)`

```{r q3}
# Guidance code:
# deg_only <- deg_res %>% filter(DEG != "NotSig")
# dim(deg_only)
# deg_only %>% arrange(desc(logFC)) %>% head(5)

# TODO
```

## Short Answer

In 2 to 3 sentences, explain:

- what the `DEG` column means
- why we classify genes into `Up`, `Down`, and `NotSig` before plotting
